Document:
# Hypertorus Fusion Reactor
The hypertorus fusion reactor is a multiblock fusion engine built around a central core. It can produce a lot of power, but it is very sensitive to heat, gas mix, and power handling.
## Precautions
[bold][color=red]DO NOT POWER THE HFR UNTIL THE FRAME, COOLING, AND INPUT LINES ARE READY.[/color][/bold]
Before starting the reactor, keep these points in mind:
• Build the machine exactly as shown below.
• Do not start the reactor without a prepared cooling loop.
• The machine consumes 100 kW while running, and cooling stops during a power outage.
• The reactor cannot be dismantled or powered off while its power level is above 0.
• Waste gases cannot be removed while the power level is above 5.
• Only helium and antinoblium can be removed from the fusion mix.
• Hypernoblium lowers mix power, while antinoblium raises it sharply.
• High heat capacity gases are harder to heat or cool.
• Low heat capacity gases are easier to heat or cool.
## Reactor Parts
The console powers the reactor on and lets you manage its controls. The core is the center of the fusion process. The fuel input feeds the reaction mix. The moderator input is used to tune the reaction. The waste output removes usable waste gases and byproducts. The corner pieces complete the multiblock frame.
## Operating Instructions
• Build the machine as shown above.
• Create a 50/50 mix of hydrogen and tritium, totaling roughly 2000 moles.
• Fill the cooling loop with plasma or hypernoblium and cool it further with space or freezers if needed.
• Connect the fuel mix to the fuel injector port and only allow about 1000 moles into the machine at first.
• Set the Heat Conductor to 500 when starting the reaction, then lower it to 100 once the power level exceeds 1.
• If the reaction starts to run hot, raise the Heat Conductor and Current Damper, reduce fuel injection, and adjust the Magnetic Constrictors until the heat output turns negative.
• Strengthen cooling and use a high heat capacity moderator gas when the reactor becomes unstable.
## Products
Depending on the chosen fuel mix, the HFR can produce helium, nitrogen, carbon dioxide, water vapor, pluoxium, anti-noblium, plasma, oxygen, BZ, hyper-noblium, proto-nitrate, zauker, healium, and ammonia.
## Summary
The HFR is best treated as a controlled fusion system rather than a simple power machine. Keep the cooling loop stable, manage the fuel and moderator carefully, and watch the console closely before increasing output.
